Argentina.note: FLAG: Active 2026 labor reform (Senate-passed) moves opposite direction from worker protection -- explicitly classifies platform workers as independent contractors under Civil/Commercial Code, excluded from Ley de Contrato de Trabajo, with no collective bargaining, no severance, no traditional labor rights. This is deregulation, not a protective platform-worker statute. Recommend downgrading composite outlook to reflect this; ratification of an ILO worker-protective convention is unlikely while this domestic deregulatory framework is being actively pursued.

ratification.text: Argentina has been an ILO member since 28.06.1919 and has ratified 84 of the 193 ILO conventions adopted to date.

legislation.text: FLAGGED: 2026 Senate-passed reform reclassifies platform workers as independent contractors under Civil/Commercial Code, excludes them from Ley de Contrato de Trabajo. This is deregulation, not protective legislation. C3 downgraded 0.5->0.0; ratification assessed as unlikely given this domestic policy direction.
legislation.note: FLAGGED: 2026 Senate-passed reform reclassifies platform workers as independent contractors under Civil/Commercial Code, excludes them from Ley de Contrato de Trabajo.
